channel.title: The Crowd Signal — The Calibration
channel.description: Short, regular reads on what prediction markets just repriced and why it matters. Analytics only — not betting advice. From The Calibration, a scored prediction-market terminal.

item.title: #005 — A conviction cools, oil gets repriced
item.description: The crowd sharply repriced crude oil lower and let a Bitcoin-sale conviction ease — a clean counterpoint to last brief's "convictions building". Plus the #004 scorecard.

item.title: #004 — Convictions vs dismissals (and we start scoring ourselves)
item.description: Where the crowd is building conviction versus dismissing risk — and the public Scoreboard opens, logging every call to be Brier-scored when its market resolves.

item.title: #003 — The crowd is pricing disruption, not collapse
item.description: Reading the difference between markets pricing disruption and markets pricing collapse — and why the distinction matters for how you read the headlines.

item.title: #002 — Fewer cuts, a faster exit: the crowd re-rated the calendar
item.description: The crowd re-rated the timeline: fewer 2026 Fed cuts priced in, and a faster political exit. What moved on the calendar, described plainly.

item.title: #001 — The week the crowd moved the timeline, not the outcome
item.description: The first Crowd Signal: a week where prediction markets shifted when something happens more than whether it happens. Sets the quality bar for the series.
